React v19.2 Learn Reference Community Blog

React Reference Overview

This section provides detailed reference documentation for working with React. For an introduction to React, please visit the Learn section instead. The React reference documentation is broken down into functional subsections so you can find the API you need quickly.

React

Programmatic React features. Hooks let you use different React features from your components. You can either use the built-in Hooks or combine them to build your own. Components let you split the UI into independent, reusable pieces, and think about each piece in isolation. A component is a function that returns markup describing what should appear on the screen.

Hooks

Hooks let you use state and other React features in your components without writing a class. State Hooks such as useState and useReducer let a component remember information like user input. Context Hooks such as useContext let a component receive information from distant parents without passing it as props. Ref Hooks such as useRef let a component hold some information that isn't used for rendering, like a DOM node or a timeout ID. Effect Hooks let a component connect to and synchronize with external systems. This includes dealing with the network, the browser DOM, animations, widgets written using a different UI library, and other non-React code.

Components

React exposes a few built-in components that you can use in your JSX. The Fragment component, often written as empty angle brackets, lets you group elements without a wrapper node. The Suspense component lets you display a fallback while child components are loading. The Profiler component lets you measure rendering performance of a React tree programmatically. These components form the foundation of how you compose a React application from smaller building blocks.

APIs

These APIs let you create components and interact with React on a deeper level. The createContext API lets you define and provide context to the child components, commonly used together with the useContext Hook. The forwardRef API lets your component expose a DOM node as a ref to a parent. The lazy API lets you defer loading a component's code until it is rendered for the first time, improving the initial load performance of your application.
